Discord/Channels/Discussion/announcement.title: announcements
Discord/Channels/Discussion/announcement.content: This is for announcements about the project. Only moderators may speak in here.

Discord/Channels/Discussion/general.title: general
Discord/Channels/Discussion/general.content:
This is for messages about the project not necessarily about the current game that is taking place.
It’s corresponding VC channel is for the same purpose.
See meeting for discussions about the game.

Discord/Channels/Game/commands.title: commands
Discord/Channels/Game/commands.content: The commands channel is for people to put in their personal commands to SussyBot and receive actions. Note that for most commands only you can see the result.

Discord/Channels/Game/control.title: control
Discord/Channels/Game/control.content: The control channel is for announcements about the game and nobody except moderators and SussyBot may speak in it.

Discord/Channels/Game/ghosts.title: ghosts
Discord/Channels/Game/ghosts.content: Ghosts is a place where only ghosts can talk about the current game and any events that are happening.

Discord/Channels/Game/meeting.title: meeting
Discord/Channels/Game/meeting.content:
This channel is to discuss the current game that is happening or any kind of important info respective for that game.
It’s corresponding VC channel is for the same purpose.

Discord/Channels/Suggestions/config-poll.title: config-polling
Discord/Channels/Suggestions/config-poll.content:
The Config polling is for polling about new configuration options or changing the current configuration options.
Things that can be changed are stuff like the roles that are in the game and their percentage and the configurations about the roles and tasks and stuff.

Discord/Channels/Suggestions/ideas.title: ideas
Discord/Channels/Suggestions/ideas.content: Ideas is a creative space for anyone to make a post (it is a Forum channel) and get suggestions on whether it should be added or not. New roles can be added via voting in law-polling.

Discord/Channels/Suggestions/law-polling.title: law-polling
Discord/Channels/Suggestions/law-polling.content: This channel is for polling about new laws to change the core features of this wiki and this game.

Discord/Commands/Codes.title: Codes
Discord/Commands/Codes.content:
codes
This command does the same thing as Role mostly, but it also gives the codes for any in-school interactions that can take place. Set as the back side of the card, it also gives non-local commands as well.
It gives you a list of the codes and which interaction they play. Make sure not to show this to people because then they know your role. Simply dish out the codes to make an interaction.

Discord/Commands/Give.title: Give
Discord/Commands/Give.content:
give [code] [target]
This dishes out a code to a certain target reporting that you gave it to them.

Discord/Commands/Help.title: Help
Discord/Commands/Help.content:
help
Sends a link to this wiki.

Discord/Commands/Moderator/Config.title: Config
Discord/Commands/Moderator/Config.content:
config [variable] [new_value]
Changes the configuration of the game. Changing the role percentage is as easy as writing the name of the role.

Discord/Commands/Moderator/Create-Task.title: Create Task
Discord/Commands/Moderator/Create-Task.content:
create_task [task_hint] [task_answer]
This creates a task and adds it to the pool and can only be run by moderators.

Discord/Commands/Moderator/Kick.title: Kick
Discord/Commands/Moderator/Kick.content:
kick [player_name]
Kicks the player from the current active game. Don’t worry! Kicked players may ready up for other games in the future.

Discord/Commands/Moderator/Start.title: Start
Discord/Commands/Moderator/Start.content:
start
Starts a game and assigns roles to each player.
After this command is given, the Role, Codes and Task commands are run for each player.

Discord/Commands/Msg.title: Msg
Discord/Commands/Msg.content:
msg [target] [message]
This messages through the bot only to the target specified.

Discord/Commands/Play.title: Play
Discord/Commands/Play.content:
play
Allows you entry to start playing the game and be considered for rounds. It is essentially a “ready up” but if you are not readied you cannot play. Make sure you are in before Start is called!

Discord/Commands/Profile.title: Profile
Discord/Commands/Profile.content:
profile [username|color|stats] ?[value]
Profile allows you to edit/view your profile.
It is like a profile customization mini app.
You change pfp (among us character), view stats, etc.
profile stats lets you see your profile formatted.
profile username [value] lets you change your username server-wide.
profile color [value] lets you change the color of your name in the server and profile of your little Among Us character that is shown.
Certain data of stats is stored like:

how many games you’ve won/lost

Discord/Commands/Quit.title: Quit
Discord/Commands/Quit.content:
quit
Allows you to quit the game.

Discord/Commands/Read-Config.title: Read Config
Discord/Commands/Read-Config.content:
read_config
This command reads the entirety of the config file for users to see.

Discord/Commands/Receive.title: Receive
Discord/Commands/Receive.content:
receive [code]
Using the Tokens API, you can report your code in which room using the Receive command.

Discord/Commands/Report.title: Report
Discord/Commands/Report.content:
report [player_name] [description]
Reports a player and broadcasts it to control where moderators can determine if the player has abused game rules.
If indeed they did, they will be kicked and won’t be able to play until next round.

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Ask.title: Ask
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Ask.content:
ask [question] ?[target]
The Medium may ask a dead player a question.
They are allowed to choose whether to ask someone in particular or ask all the ghosts.
The Medium may ask a question by choosing from a dropdown of acceptable questions.
Ghosts may respond by clicking a dropdown of acceptable answers.
Even if the question is broadly asked, the player ghost who answered the question is displayed in the personal announcement.
Note also that ghosts can lie.
Questions and Answers List
What role were you?
Here the ghost may select from any role.
Where did you die?
Here the ghost may select from many dropdowns:
WING: A, B, C, etc.
FLOOR: 1, 2
ROOM: 107, 109, etc.
or/and
NAME: Library, Gym, Hallway, etc.
or/and
IN-MEETING, OUT OF SCHOOL(custom name)
Which role killed you?
Here the ghost may select from any role, including “I’m not sure.”
How many tasks did you complete before you died?
Here the ghost can type a number.
Did anyone else witness your death?
Yes / No / I’m not sure / It happened very privately
Did you trust your killer before you died?
Yes / No / I had no suspicions / I didn’t know who my killer was
Would you have won if you survived?
Definitely / Probably / Unlikely / No chance

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Douse.title: Douse
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Douse.content:
douse [image]
This allows the Arsonist to report a dousing event and they must do so by the same time everyone submits their tokens.

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Guess.title: Guess
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Guess.content:
guess [player] [role]
Allows you to guess someone’s role if you are Evil Guesser or Nice Guesser.
A wrong guess will result in your death.

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Hack.title: Hack
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Hack.content:
hack
This command hacks the system for Hacker and gives information about the system.

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Protect.title: Protect
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Protect.content:
protect [player]
As a Guardian Angel, you can protect someone before the day begins. This protection lasts for the day.

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Reroll.title: Reroll
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Reroll.content:
reroll [player] ?[num]
The reroll command for the Engineer rerolls the task of someone. If the number isn’t specified, a random task is rerolled.

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Revive.title: Revive
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Revive.content:
revive [player]
The revive command initiates the revive action for the Altruist and revives a player at a cost.

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Sabotage.title: Sabotage
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Sabotage.content:
sabotage [player]
Under the Saboteur, allows the giving of an additional task towards a player and does not require locality.

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Silence.title: Silence
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Silence.content:
silence [player_name]
Server mutes & prevents a player from speaking. Please note that this player may still use commands except voting.

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Swap.title: Swap
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Swap.content:
swap [first_name] [second_name]
Swaps the votes on two players.

Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Task.title: Task
Discord/Commands/Role-Specific/Task.content:
task
To view a task, run task and it will show all of your tasks numbered, and any role-specific ability information like the Snitch, Medium, or Swapper.
task [num] [answer]
To complete a task, enter the number of the task you are completing and the answer to your task. You may keep trying to complete the task as many times as needed if you get it wrong.

Discord/Commands/Role.title: Role
Discord/Commands/Role.content:
role [?name]
Tells you your role and gives you a cool image corresponding to your role.
If you are a Ghost, you can also find the roles of other players by mentioning their name afterwards.
Config:
ghosts_know: bool = true: Whether ghosts can use this command to find your role.

Discord/Commands/Vitals.title: Vitals
Discord/Commands/Vitals.content:
vitals
This command pulls up the vitals. It is automatically pulled up after the Tokens of the day are registered. This is essentially the area where the meeting takes place like what would happen in a regular Among Us game. You can who people have voted, if they are cursed, stuff like that.

Discord/Commands/Vote.title: Vote
Discord/Commands/Vote.content:
vote [player/skip] ?[player_name]
The Vote command allows you to vote for the current day.
Votes may also be skipped for the current day.
Once you vote, you cannot go back.

Discord/Control-Flow.title: Control Flow
Discord/Control-Flow.content:
At any time, anyone may question anything.
This is the basis for which many of my projects run.
Polls control the narrative, and all polls are based on mutually-exclusive approval voting.
Rather than voting for one option of a crowd, vote for ALL OPTIONS YOU APPROVE OF.
People are free to make polls at any time on any matter, but it helps to be organized so try to send them in their respective channels.
If old votes conflict with new votes, the new votes are favored.
The result of a poll is whatever option gets the most votes.
If several options tie, they are considered all acceptable and whoever made the poll gets to decide.

Discord/Discord-Roles/Moderator.title: Moderator
Discord/Discord-Roles/Moderator.content: This is a special role for Moderators - they can see everything, even private threads which is why this is reserved only for the bot.

Discord/Discord-Roles/Player-Moderator.title: Player Moderator
Discord/Discord-Roles/Player-Moderator.content: A player moderator has all the same abilities as a Moderator, except it cannot see private threads. In addition to that, it cannot see ghost chat.

Discord/Discord-Roles/Project-OG-Whiz.title: Project OG Whiz
Discord/Discord-Roles/Project-OG-Whiz.content: This is a cosmetic role signifying that the player has previously been a part of one of Mason’s projects including but not limited to the modded Minecraft server PZ.

Discord/Discord-Roles/ghost.title: ghost
Discord/Discord-Roles/ghost.content: Ghosts are a marker role assigned by SussyBot to mark a player that is dead. Ghosts may speak in the special ghosts channel.

Discord/Discord-Roles/player.title: player
Discord/Discord-Roles/player.content: Players are a marker role to signify that the person is in an active game.

Discord/SussyBot-Game-Data.title: SussyBot Game Data
Discord/SussyBot-Game-Data.content:
SussyBot stores certain data about the games that occur.
In-between games and persistent per-server, SussyBot stores:
List of Data persistent Between Games:

Config config
Profile profile user
Tasks tasks id

And of course there is data stored during games, about the roles, tasks everybody has completed, etc. there is a lot and for this you should consult the actual code (don’t worry it should be pretty readable!)
Here are some important default values:
Configs are assigned how they are represented in each respective file.
Profiles are based off the discord profile first until they are chosen to change.
Tasks are initially empty and must be mass added by the server.

Discord/SussyBot.title: SussyBot
Discord/SussyBot.content:
SussyBot is the Discord Bot that can handle all of the Commands that are given.
Prefix your command with / to activate it.

Mechanics/Game-Control-Flow.title: Game Control Flow
Mechanics/Game-Control-Flow.content:
COMMANDS via SussyBot are the staple of this project as it allows a central server for the game to be run on.
This game is designed with the spirit of preventing as many abuses as possible, but if an abuse comes up, make sure to do the Report command.
Before a game can be started, players must join up with the Play command.
The Game starts with a Start command.
Rounds last as long as is necessary until someone wins.
Each person is given a role where they will have a certain win condition.
For the Crewmates to win, all tasks must be completed or all killing roles must be voted out or eliminated.
For the Imposters to win, they must have killed all players.
Neutrals have their own goal specific to their role.
Crewmates win together and Imposters win together whereas typically Neutrals will earn solo wins, but not always.
Interactions during the school day are tracked using Tokens. All exchanged Tokens must be reported using Receive and Give by 4:00.
At 4:00, the events of the day are reported and everyone can see what has happened.
If you are killed, you are turned into a ghost. A ghost may not leak information about the game to other players but get a dedicated space in the Discord and can also piece together the mystery or do /role to figure out the role of somebody. PLEASE don’t ruin the game by leaking information as to who is what role. This is seen as one of the only serious offenses and can get you banned from future games.
After the day is up, at 7:00 to account for people who have athletic activities or other after-school commitments, the meeting time begins. It is designed to last at most 30 minutes with on average maybe about 10 minutes, but it can last for however long is necessary.
The aim of the meeting is to vote out a player or skip vote.
The days end at 10:00, where no more voting can be done and the results of the day are recorded.
That’s all, have fun!!! :D
To learn more, please read MECHANICS and ROLES so that you can understand how certain mechanisms are done.
IN ESSENCE TLDR:
There are only TWO RULES:

Don’t exchange codes if you haven’t interacted with a player.
If you are dead, don’t expose anything.

Config
game_duration: u8 = 5: Duration of the game in days.

Mechanics/Tasks.title: Tasks
Mechanics/Tasks.content:
Tasks are implemented by finding certain signs around the building and filling the blanks verbatim with what they say.
To complete or view your tasks, you can use the Task interface.
Example
For example, you can be given five tasks that hint to a location or have you fill in the blanks.
Like:
At GHS, we are ___, kind, and compassionate.
The task would be finding the sign and what goes in that location.
Config
task_amt: u8 = 7: Amount of tasks Crewmates are given to complete.

Mechanics/Tokens.title: Tokens
Mechanics/Tokens.content:
Interaction Tokens or Tokens for short are a system where, when you interact, you exchange a token. You write on a piece of paper or anything and give them a code to interact and cause an Action.
The tokens are 6-digits long and alphanumeric. They are given to you by the Codes command when everyone has started. Each code does something special which is also stated per role.
Tokens are created to preserve LOCALITY. Exchanging Tokens means you have to be close to the target. These are essential for all imposters, some neutrals, and some crewmates.
There are only two regulating rules:

Do not exchange codes you’ve received/given with other people.
Digitally exchanging codes does not count.

At the end of the day, codes must be reported via the following commands along with some information:
Give, if you are giving a code.
Receive, if you are receiving a code.

Mechanics/Voting.title: Voting
Mechanics/Voting.content:
Players may vote using the Vote command.
Votes are cast once per day.
All players regardless of role may vote unless otherwise specified.
Once you vote, you cannot revoke it, so make sure you vote before the day ends!
Configuration
colors: bool: Determines whether or not to show colors of players after voting.
confirmation: bool: Determines whether or not to show a confirmation message that the player was/was not an imposter.

Roles/Crewmate/Altruist.title: Altruist
Roles/Crewmate/Altruist.content:
The Altruist is a crewmate that can sacrifice certain freedoms in order to revive players from the game. The Altruist gets two chances/stages.
Each time the Altruist revives a player, it is announced that the player will be back in the game.
They can use the Revive command.
The Altruist may revive the first player by sacrificing their freedom to speak in Discord chat and meetings. After the first revival, they are server muted, but can still discuss in person and can still vote, as opposed to the effects of the Blackmailer.
The second revival results in the cost of revealing for the Altruist. It may not sound that bad, but if Evil Guesser is enabled and they are not out yet, it could mean certain death.
Config
free_revival: bool = false: Determines whether the Altruist gets a free revival before the two take into effect, totaling to three total.
task_barrier: Option<u8> = None: Determines whether they need to have completed a certain number of tasks before they may initiate revivals.

Roles/Crewmate/Crewmate.title: Crewmate
Roles/Crewmate/Crewmate.content:
Crewmates is the generic role for a nice guy for the benefit of the ship.
Crewmates can do Tasks in order to win and also have some cool bonus roles.

Roles/Crewmate/Deputy.title: Deputy
Roles/Crewmate/Deputy.content: As a powerful counter to the Eraser and essential to work with the Sheriff, the Deputy can handcuff certain players preventing them from using their ability using the local Interaction Tokens.

Roles/Crewmate/Engineer.title: Engineer
Roles/Crewmate/Engineer.content:
The Engineer can reroll other people’s tasks if they are having a rough time and also remove tasks from team mates as a counter to the Saboteur. The engineer can do these actions at max three times combined per game.
To reroll someone’s task they can use the Reroll command,
while they are given an optional token to use in order to remove tasks from someone.
If the target is not a crewmate, this does nothing.

Roles/Crewmate/Guardian-Angel.title: Guardian Angel
Roles/Crewmate/Guardian-Angel.content:
When a Crewmate dies, they have a chance of becoming a Guardian Angel. Guardian Angels can protect one person per day before the day starts using the Protect command.
Config:
pct: f32 = 0.2: Percent of a Crewmate becoming a Guardian Angel when they die.

Roles/Crewmate/Hacker.title: Hacker
Roles/Crewmate/Hacker.content:
The Hacker can leak the interaction codes given after 4:00 by using the Hack command. More specifically, they can see who interacted with who and what time the code was submitted.
Config
random_nerf: bool = true: Whether to integrate the random noise present in hacking the system.

Roles/Crewmate/Mayor.title: Mayor
Roles/Crewmate/Mayor.content:
The Mayor has double the Voting power.
In other words, voting for somebody is as if two people voted for them.
Not only this, but they can see colors of votes after meetings.

Roles/Crewmate/Medic.title: Medic
Roles/Crewmate/Medic.content: The Medic is given a token to shield a target from an attack. The shield applies for the rest of the game. If the Medic dies, the shield disappears with them.

Roles/Crewmate/Medium.title: Medium
Roles/Crewmate/Medium.content:
The Medium is a Crewmate that can speak to the dead. Using the Ask command, it can ask a question to ghosts by picking from a list of acceptable questions.
Like the Swapper, the medium has an amount of “asks” that it gains after completing each task which is tracked via the bot responding after completing a task.

Roles/Crewmate/Nice-Guesser.title: Nice Guesser
Roles/Crewmate/Nice-Guesser.content:
With the Guess command, it can guess a player’s role at any time and kill them.
If you guess the wrong role, you die.

Roles/Crewmate/Prosecutor.title: Prosecutor
Roles/Crewmate/Prosecutor.content:
The Prosecutor is a crewmate that is almost a nice counter to the Blackmailer.
They have the ability to “prosecute” a player exiling them from meeting, using the Silence command.

Roles/Crewmate/Sheriff.title: Sheriff
Roles/Crewmate/Sheriff.content:
The Sheriff has the ability to try to kill someone by taking a shot. They can exchange their SHOT code. If the target is an imposter/neutral, they die, but if they are a crewmate, you die.
Config
neutrals_set: Vec<Role>: the set of neutral roles that count as a valid imposter guess. Used also by the Thief.

Roles/Crewmate/Snitch.title: Snitch
Roles/Crewmate/Snitch.content:
The Snitch, through completing tasks, gains information about the players over time in three stages.
Stage 1: (\frac{1}{3} of Tasks)
After reaching stage 1, typically after 2 tasks, the Snitch is given a list of players where at least one of which is neutral.
Stage 2: (\frac{2}{3} of Tasks)
After reaching stage 2, typically after 4 tasks, the Snitch is given a list of players where at least one of which is a killer.
Stage 3: Final Task
After reaching the final stage by completing the final task, you are given the amount of letters in the username of an imposter. However, you are also revealed to the imposters that you are the Snitch.
Config
list_size: u8 = 3: The size of the list of people given.

Roles/Crewmate/Spy.title: Spy
Roles/Crewmate/Spy.content: The Spy appears as an Imposter to other Imposters but cannot kill.

Roles/Crewmate/Swapper.title: Swapper
Roles/Crewmate/Swapper.content:
The Swapper is a Crewmate role that allows the ability to Swap the votes of people by the end of the day.
The Swapper gains one “swap” every time they complete a task. A swapper cannot use swap unless they have completed at least one task.
Config
have_swaps: bool = true: Whether to gatekeep swapping behind tasks.

Roles/Impostor/Blackmailer.title: Blackmailer
Roles/Impostor/Blackmailer.content:
The Blackmailer can choose one target before the meeting to prevent from speaking (server mute & silence) once per day. The target also is not allowed to vote.
It uses the Silence command.

Roles/Impostor/Bounty-Hunter.title: Bounty Hunter
Roles/Impostor/Bounty-Hunter.content:
The Bounty Hunter gets a specific target and can kill only that target. Once the target is killed, two new ones are given the following day.
Specifically, they are given a code that can only be used to kill the target. The number of targets they kill successfully they earn that number more kill tokens.
Config
new_targets: u8 = 2: How many new targets are given after a target is killed.
non_bounty_max_kill: u8 = 0: How many players can be killed unrelated to the given target per day.

Roles/Impostor/Eraser.title: Eraser
Roles/Impostor/Eraser.content: The Eraser is an imposter that can essentially erase the impact of certain codes in Roles like killing, medic shields, etc. . It can also disable actions from non-local actors like Guessers.

Roles/Impostor/Evil-Guesser.title: Evil Guesser
Roles/Impostor/Evil-Guesser.content:
With the Guess command, it can guess a player’s role at any time and kill them.
If you guess the wrong role, you die.

Roles/Impostor/Imposter.title: Imposter
Roles/Impostor/Imposter.content:
This is the base role for an imposter.
All Imposter roles can automatically kill unless explicitly stated otherwise.
The imposter can kill only one person per day.
Killing is implemented by Interaction Tokens.
Config
max_kill: u8 = 1: The maximum amount of an imposter can kill in one day.

Roles/Impostor/Saboteur.title: Saboteur
Roles/Impostor/Saboteur.content:
As an evil counter to the Engineer essentially, the Saboteur is able to give more tasks to the players, up to 4 extra tasks towards players via the Sabotage command.
If the tasks are given to a non-crewmate, nothing happens.
Config
tasks: u8 = 4: Amount of new tasks are able to be assigned.

Roles/Impostor/Witch.title: Witch
Roles/Impostor/Witch.content:
The Witch can Curse players by exchanging a code.
The Witch may only curse at most one player per day.
A cursed player dies after the most recent Group Meeting, at the end of the day/start of the next day unless the Witch isn’t alive (voted out or died in meeting or beforehand).
Config
cursed_count: u8 = 1: Amount of players that can be cursed per day.

Roles/Neutral/Arsonist.title: Arsonist
Roles/Neutral/Arsonist.content:
In a similar spirit to the game, this role must get pictures of everybody alive and then send them as proof all at once in order to win.
As opposed to tokens, you must submit this as the Arsonist using the Douse command.
In the real game, the Arsonist must douse everyone (basically touch them) and then click a button to win, but this is an adaption for the game.

Roles/Neutral/Jackal.title: Jackal
Roles/Neutral/Jackal.content: The Jackal is an independent killing role and can only win alone. It’s goal is to kill everyone but works independently of the Imposters. If this sounds weird, note that neutral killers and even imposters can kill imposters, because of the Spy.

Roles/Neutral/Jester.title: Jester
Roles/Neutral/Jester.content:
The Jester wins simply by being voted off.
Try to be sus!

Roles/Neutral/Lawyer.title: Lawyer
Roles/Neutral/Lawyer.content:
With your role you are given a target.
The lawyer wins with their client who can be either Neutral or Imposter but has no special abilities. Your client cannot confirm (doesn’t know) whether you are their Lawyer.
You can tell your client, but will they believe you?

Roles/Neutral/Thief.title: Thief
Roles/Neutral/Thief.content: The Thief is the same as the Sheriff except if you kill target, you become that role, otherwise you die.

Roles/Neutral/Vampire.title: Vampire
Roles/Neutral/Vampire.content:
Using Tokens, the Vampire can infect someone with one token per day and they have a chance of becoming another vampire and their win condition changes to infect.
Config
chance: f32 = 0.2: Chance of becoming a vampire after an infect.

Roles/Neutral/Vulture.title: Vulture
Roles/Neutral/Vulture.content:
The vulture can predict the death of somebody and eat their corpse in doing so. The Vulture needs to eat a percentage of the bodies in order to win.
Config
win_pct: f32 = 0.2: How much of the players they must predict.

Wiki/Brainstorming/Brainstorming-Passage.title: Brainstorming Passages
Wiki/Brainstorming/Brainstorming-Passage.content:
Water Wars
Special Water Wars is an EXCLUSIVLY school game rather than water wars which is outside of school.
Reason: many are worried about prices, etc,
also its fun and i thought of this one day independent of water wars when hunting down somebody,
after that I knew to do it
Medium
the medium can speak to ghosts by getting certain hints about the roles of players or how someone died- what role or which room or something? Yea the point is that you speak to the ghosts so the ghosts should be able to access a dropdown and interactive or be able to select from a range of where they were killed maybe they get an amount of questions based on their tasks
medium answers must not be broadcasted for a reason - we don’t want players to know evidence for sure from an anonymous medium - if the medium wants to reveal information they must also reveal their role
Snitch
the snitch is kinda a combo with the seer
Seer, get roles clues
Role of Moderators
You can also include a moderator for more advanced things like the proof from the Arsonist or maybe verifying certain pictures.
Add some way people can fight back if they determine there was a wrong kill request or something - handled by Report
Notes
Do we even want to do this?
IDK - sounded better in our heads we have a lot less imposters and how would this game even scale with tons of people?
Well - why not give it a shot! Even if it is niche, no issue!! Its just gonna be challenging to go through this if there isn’t much participation on the role suggestion side  - what am I worrying about? let’s do this thing!!!

definitely a weird role and in my opinion is not fun but this one can be added for sure, (but i added a twist to it)
maybe like a deputy but for evil

Handcuff ability, eraser can be evil one

Sticky note as shield

Verifications and Actions (Pre-Tokens)

^ their sent signature like their sent role if you don’t believe them
Digital Verifications are generated by the discord bot and these can be used to verify someone’s role in the case of killing someone in the game
Tasks
You can implement tasks by having certain hints to find certain texts and then when they input the text verbatim the task is completed. You can be given/generated a map, too.
Don’t worry about enforcing these rules as we can write a Discord bot to do it.

and make crewmate role that can make imposters to task, maybe taskmaster or engineer?

Wiki/Brainstorming/Curse.title: Curse
Wiki/Brainstorming/Curse.content:
curse [player_name] [picture]
In order to curse somebody, you must send their picture.
This command will broadcast anonymously to the discord who was cursed in the control channel.
This was created before the Tokens system and has now been replaced.
However, the Arsonist still does this.
by taking a picture of them.
The picture must be included in the Curse to count.
But be careful - everyone can see the image as a clue to who was cursed.

Wiki/Brainstorming/Shifting-of-Actions-M.title: Shifting of Actions Mechanics
Wiki/Brainstorming/Shifting-of-Actions-M.content:
this idea was basically to shift actions mechanics earlier so the medic would have to say who they wanted to protect beforehand - basically stuff that broke locality but this new system is tons better
Action Preferring, prereference - kind of like deferring but for actions and before and it basically allows you to prevent the in-game action of something during rounds by instead asking you to do it in general before a certain date beforehand
Now we have the Actions and Locality Tokens system.

Wiki/Command-Format.title: Command Format
Wiki/Command-Format.content:
Commands are formatted by first naming the command and then parameters are listed afterwards.
Parameter types may be specified with : and are surrounded by [].
? means an optional parameter and / allows a combination of accepted values.

Wiki/Configuration.title: Configuration
Wiki/Configuration.content:
Certain roles have configurations that will be mentioned in their role and so do certain mechanics.
: means type.
= means default value.
: Outside of the parameter defines the point of the configuration.

Wiki/FAQ.title: FAQ
Wiki/FAQ.content:
Can I play both Senior Assassins Official and this game?
Absolutely, yeah! This game is free to enter, free to play, and free to enjoy for everyone - it is not a mandatory alternative intended to take attention away from the game but to be a fun and safe game that everyone can enjoy in addition to all else!!
Once I am killed, can I talk about it with active players?
Technically, nothing is preventing ghosts from discussing with active crew members, however, this is pretty much the only large bannable/kickable offense and is an incredibly discouraged practice as it sells the game and results in cheating.
If you want to discuss, however, don’t hesitate to discuss in the exclusive ghosts channel! Not to mention all the ways you can help out through players like the Medium!!
Can I give people fake cards with codes?
Yes, absolutely! This is what makes it sneaky! You can fake an interaction by faking a code and giving it to somebody. The code won’t do anything, but it will trick the player! As long as the rules of the codes are not broken and everything is submitted, anything can be done!!
Can I collaborate on tasks with Crewmates?
Absolutely you can - just make sure you know who it is you are trusting! You can lie and do all that you normally would in a normal Among Us game as well. It’s even better if you work together like the Medium, Snitch, and Swapper altogether make a crazy combo. The roles are strongest when they are together - leverage that.
What happens during the weekend, if anything?
If rounds happen during the weekend, which they can, optional meetings may be held. Ultimately, we’ll figure it out.
How does a kill work? Can’t somebody lie?
Technically, yes, someone can lie, but that is with all things like water wars, too.
But ultimately the imposter must send a /give command in order to register the kill. If this is not done by the end of school (4:00), it’s not valid. Alternatively, the victim can report their death with the /receive command by the end of school. In any event, their death will be posted by the end of school, allowing us to meet.
If the imposter killed illegally without showing the target the code or reporting it, the kill is not valid.
As a moderator playing the game, can you see ghost chat?
Yes, unfortunately, it is true I can see ghost chat. If you have something private to say, you can use the Msg command through the bot then specify the Role ghost to send it only to ghosts, but I promise I will not check ghost chat at all when alive.

Wiki/Marker-Roles.title: Marker Roles
Wiki/Marker-Roles.content: Marker roles are roles that are used by SussyBot to store information about the state of the player. It is necessary in order to direct discord about which players of which state are allowed in which channel. Marker roles can be seen by anybody and are frequent to change and are signified by the all lowercase/snakecase name.

index.title: index
index.content:
Welcome to the Wet Vents wiki!
Wet Vents is a new Water Works/Senior Assassins idea with a fusion in Among Us.
It is a school-based game rather than water wars which is an outside-of-school based game. This makes it plenty safer, plenty more fun with tons more interaction, and
To learn about the game, see Game Control Flow.
To learn about the Discord and how it works, see DISCORD.
MECHANICS are specific, distinguishing parts of the game that players must know.
MODIFIERS are special rules that apply to groups of players with certain roles and can be randomly assigned.
ROLES are randomly assigned goals to certain players tied to their win condition.
See FAQ for any quick questions you may have.
TLDR:
Wet Vents is an in-person Among Us with games lasting a week where after each day we meet on Discord and can determine the imposters or evil roles. Players are killed by showing a certain symbol and your goal is to survive as a crewmate, sabotage as an imposter, or chase an independent goal with as a neutral.
